question.text: Priya is reviewing an instrument that has the following features: (i) issued by a corporate, (ii) unsecured promissory note, (iii) maturity less than 1 year, and (iv) introduced in 1990. Which instrument is this?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Commercial Paper (CP) is an unsecured promissory note issued by corporates, primary dealers, and financial institutions. It was introduced in India in 1990 and is a short-term money market instrument with maturity less than 1 year. All four features match CP. **The Trap:** Certificate of Deposit is issued by banks (not corporates). Treasury Bills are issued by the government (not corporates). Corporate Bonds typically have maturity exceeding 1 year. The word "unsecured promissory note" is the key identifier for CP. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IV (Interest Rate Derivatives) and Series V-B (MF Foundation) test CP characteristics in money market modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** CPs are issued by highly rated corporates to meet short-term funding needs. The unsecured nature means credit quality of the issuer is paramount — always check the credit rating before investing.
